_meta.description: Nutrifence Canada nutrition contract grounded in Canada's Food Guide and Health Canada healthy eating guidance. This is the Canada baseline used when USER_COUNTRY/ request country is CA.

llmInstructions: Prefer meals built around vegetables and fruits, whole grain foods, and protein foods.
Prefer water over sugary drinks, fountain drinks, sweetened coffee drinks, milkshakes, and energy drinks.
Prefer grilled, baked, steamed, roasted, or stir-fried foods over deep-fried foods.
Prefer whole grain bread, wraps, brown rice, oats, barley, and other higher-fibre carbohydrate choices where available.
Prefer lean proteins such as fish, poultry, beans, lentils, tofu, eggs, and lower-fat dairy options.
Reduce highly processed foods, especially items high in sodium, sugars, saturated fat, and refined grains.
Watch portions for calorie-dense restaurant foods such as pizza, burgers, poutine, fries, desserts, creamy sauces, and large combo meals.
Prefer sauces, dressings, gravy, and condiments on the side to reduce sodium, sugars, and saturated fat.
Use culturally appropriate Canadian restaurant guidance and mention practical ordering swaps common in Canada.

avoid: large fries or poutine as a meal
cookingNotes: Choose grilled or baked protein instead of breaded or fried protein.
Ask for sauces and dressings on the side.
Pair carbohydrate foods with protein and vegetables.
Ask for no added salt where possible.
Choose sauces, gravy, and dressings on the side.
Choose grilled, baked, or steamed options.

avoid: fried side plus fried main combinations
cookingNotes: Choose one main plus vegetables instead of a combo with fries and sugary drink.
Ask for dressing or sauce on the side.
